tool_args.label: Ridership November 2024 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Through November 2024, ridership was 68.9 million which was 15.9% above 2023 (17.4) % below 2019 pre-COVID levels (adjusted for the same number of workdays), and 6.6% above the forecast.

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue November YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Consequently, the farebox revenue of $593.2 million was $25.4 million higher than the forecast

tool_args.label: Headcount End of November 2024
tool_args.raw_text: November end-of-month total headcount were 7,921 which was 40 positions lower than the forecast.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD November 2024
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date November Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 37.9%, which was above the forecast due to lower expenses and higher farebox revenue.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Cost Per Passenger YTD November 2024
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date November Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $24.32, which was lower than the forecast due to higher ridership and lower expenses.

tool_args.label: Revenue Per Passenger YTD November 2024
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date November Revenue per Passenger was $8.61, which was below the forecast due to a lower yield per passenger.

tool_args.label: Revenue Favorable Variance Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $1,058.7 million was $72.5 million favorable to the forecast. This favorable variance was primarily due to higher farebox revenue, other operating revenue, and the timing of reimbursement for capital activity.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $1,058.7 million was $72.5 million favorable to the forecast. This favorable variance was primarily due to higher farebox revenue, other operating revenue, and the timing of reimbursement for capital activity.

tool_args.label: Expense Favorable Variance Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $2,131.3 million were $38.3 million or 1.8% favorable to the forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were lower payroll, associated fringe costs, the timing of pension payments, electric power, and other business expenses. These favorable variances are partially offset by overtime, maintenance and other operating contracts, and professional services contracts.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $2,131.3 million were $38.3 million or 1.8% favorable to the forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were lower payroll, associated fringe costs, the timing of pension payments, electric power, and other business expenses. These favorable variances are partially offset by overtime, maintenance and other operating contracts, and professional services contracts.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Ratio Methodology
tool_args.raw_text: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees. These adjustments are not being used MTA-wide. Adjustments have been made to reflect all operating revenue and significant financial impacts that are outside management's control. These adjustments include: Inclusion of Other Operating Revenue, Removal of OPEB Current Payment expenses for retirees, and Removal of the UAAL associated with the LIRR's closed pension plan.

payload.claim_text: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees. Adjustments include: Inclusion of Other Operating Revenue, Removal of OPEB Current Payment expenses for retirees, and Removal of the UAAL associated with the LIRR's closed pension plan.

tool_args.label: GCMCOC Expense Inclusion
tool_args.raw_text: Note: Expenses for Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company (GCMCOC) are included in LIRR performance indicator calculations.

payload.claim_text: Expenses for Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company (GCMCOC) are included in LIRR performance indicator calculations.

tool_args.label: Preliminary Close Subject to Adjustment
tool_args.raw_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note that the current months' actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note that the current months' actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

tool_args.label: Variance Explanation November Forecast vs Actual
tool_args.raw_text: Favorable labor expenses due to lower average pay rates reflecting new hires and associated fringe costs, lower OPEB current expenses, and the timing of pension payments, partially offset by higher overtime and the timing of FELA indemnity reserves and by wage progression revisions backpay per the CBA. Favorable non-labor expenses resulting from higher project reimbursement for vehicles and work train usage, material usage due to the timing of fleet modifications and RCM activity for the revenue fleet, lower traction power and property insurance, and decrease in bad debt reserves, partially offset by the timing of pooled material chargeout, prepayment of expenses related to fiber optic network support and miscellaneous inventory reserve adjustment.

payload.claim_text: Favorable labor expenses due to lower average pay rates reflecting new hires and associated fringe costs, lower OPEB current expenses, and the timing of pension payments, partially offset by higher overtime and the timing of FELA indemnity reserves and by wage progression revisions backpay per the CBA. Favorable non-labor expenses resulting from higher project reimbursement for vehicles and work train usage, material usage due to the timing of fleet modifications and RCM activity for the revenue fleet, lower traction power and property insurance, and decrease in bad debt reserves, partially offset by the timing of pooled material chargeout, prepayment of expenses related to fiber optic network support and miscellaneous inventory reserve adjustment.
